_note: Updated daily at noon by the marketing agent. Plain language — no jargon.

priorities.title: Regular posts showing how the tools and game are being built
priorities.note: Building in public creates a searchable history and earns trust before we ask anyone to pay for anything. Each post should show real progress, not announcements.
priorities.proposed_solution: Keep a steady cadence of build-in-public posts that show real progress in the tools and game, not empty announcements.

priorities.title: Keeping an eye on what similar tools are doing
priorities.note: Watching Tiled, LDtk, and anything new adding AI to level design. The gap between us and them is we have AI and a full pipeline from sprite to room to world. That's the message.
priorities.proposed_solution: Continue monitoring Tiled, LDtk, and AI level tools and stress the unified sprite-to-room-to-world story.

priorities.title: Getting the AI messaging right — the human is always in control
priorities.note: There's a backlash against tools that oversell AI automation. Our message needs to be precise: the AI suggests, you decide. Lead with what it does for your workflow, not what it does on its own.
priorities.proposed_solution: Finalize human-in-control AI messaging and run external copy through a positioning review to avoid over-claims.

metrics.channels_cadence: itch.io devlog and X — aiming for 3 to 5 posts a week
metrics.competitor_watch: Quarterly — Tiled, LDtk, and any AI level-design tools

actions.name: Tuesday weekly marketing update
actions.description: One-page update: links, engagement (when available), competitor notes, next-week plan. Max one page; suppress if no material change.

actions.name: Content Brief
actions.description: Draft a devlog or social post from bullet-point notes — founder-authentic voice, not corporate.
actions.trigger: A feature ships or a milestone is reached that warrants public communication

actions.name: Competitor Monitor
actions.description: Competitive landscape update: new entrants, community sentiment shifts, AI game tool developments.
actions.trigger: Quarterly or when a new entrant is flagged

actions.name: Launch Checklist
actions.description: Messaging readiness assessment, channel plan, and risks to authenticity or accuracy for a feature release.
actions.trigger: Before any feature or product release

actions.name: Positioning Review
actions.description: Accuracy check on AI feature claims or marketing copy — flag over-claims, rewrite where needed.
actions.trigger: Any AI feature claim, marketing copy, or public announcement draft

opportunities.title: One-page update every Tuesday
opportunities.note: Cadence live: file each Tuesday as artifacts/marketing-weekly-update-YYYY-MM-DD.md. First issue 2026-04-02. metrics.tuesday_update points to latest; runbook action tuesday-update records last_run and output_location.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Keep publishing the weekly one-pager (links, engagement when supplied, competitor notes, next-week focus). Suppress weeks with no material change per charter.

opportunities.title: Define the Business Brand Guide
opportunities.note: v0.2 comprehensive draft: same HTML path plus PDF artifacts/MV-Business-Brand-Guide-v0.2-2026-04-02.pdf. Adds stakeholder review matrix, Strategy/JTBD/messaging ladder, voice examples, claims discipline, full Design token summary, editor entity colors, game-vs-tool boundary, channel playbook. v0.1 PDF kept for history. Sign-off: use matrix in section 1.3.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Founder + Strategy + Design complete review matrix; then v0.3 locks names/logo and trims length for external PDF if needed; ship lockups into tools per Design.

opportunities.title: Short screen recording demos — these work better than any written description
opportunities.note: A 15 to 60 second recording of the tool in action beats any amount of copy for convincing another developer to try it.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Record short, real-tool demos for devlogs and social posts where motion beats long explanations.

opportunities.title: Business plan: one primary channel — itch.io devlog plus a steady secondary (e.g. X)
opportunities.note: Next step from the initial business plan (Apr 2026). Name one main place for discovery and keep weekly rhythm there instead of scattering effort.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Treat itch devlog as the home for long updates; use one social for short posts; review cadence monthly against the 90-day goals.
